channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-13231] sanity test_901: FAIL: mgs lock leak (34 != 37)
item.description:
This issue was created by maloo for jianyu <yujian@whamcloud.com>

This issue relates to the following test suite run: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/86cd8382-4a79-11ea-b58e-52540065bddc

test_901 failed with the following error:

== sanity test 901: don't leak a mgc lock on client umount =========================================== 05:31:29 (1581168689)
CMD: trevis-17vm12 /usr/sbin/lctl get_param -n ldlm.namespaces.MGS*.lock_count
10.9.4.209@tcp:/lustre /mnt/lustre lustre rw,flock,user_xattr,lazystatfs 0 0
CMD: trevis-16vm11 grep -c /mnt/lustre' ' /proc/mounts
Stopping client trevis-16vm11 /mnt/lustre (opts:)
CMD: trevis-16vm11 lsof -t /mnt/lustre
CMD: trevis-16vm11 umount  /mnt/lustre 2>&1
Starting client: trevis-16vm11:  -o user_xattr,flock trevis-17vm12@tcp:/lustre /mnt/lustre
CMD: trevis-16vm11 mkdir -p /mnt/lustre
CMD: trevis-16vm11 mount -t lustre -o user_xattr,flock trevis-17vm12@tcp:/lustre /mnt/lustre
CMD: trevis-17vm12 /usr/sbin/lctl get_param -n ldlm.namespaces.MGS*.lock_count
 sanity test_901: @@@@@@ FAIL: mgs lock leak (34 != 37)

<<Please provide additional information about the failure here>>

VVVVVVV DO NOT REMOVE LINES BELOW, Added by Maloo for auto-association VVVVVVV
sanity test_901 - mgs lock leak (34 != 37)
item.summary: sanity test_901: FAIL: mgs lock leak (34 != 37)

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.

comments.comment:
Note, there is different cases when 'mgc lock leak' - note 'mgc' here as client locks - and 'mgs lock leak' which is about locks on server. This ticket was originally about 'mgs' but contains all test_901 failures linked after all.

First of all this doesn't hit master branch clients since 2022, all failures for 'mgs lock leak' in Maloo are about b2_14 client only. So I'd say this is b2_14 issue at the moment.

Exascale clients are failing with 'mgc lock leak 2 != 4' and that issue looks false because client has always at least 4 MGC locks - 'fsname-sptlrpc', 'fsname-client', 'fsname-cliir' and 'params', so if it reports oldcount as 2 - that is already false report.

Then I checking log from such failure, event are ordered by time:

new lock: 0000000068eb8698/0x543108aef6b151c5 lrc: 3/1,0 - sptlrpc
new lock: 000000009755086f/0x543108aef6b151cc lrc: 3/1,0 - client
new lock: 00000000e3c67f22/0x543108aef6b15219 lrc: 3/1,0 - cliir
new lock: 0000000074b7b922/0x543108aef6b15220 lrc: 3/1,0 - params

### MGC cancel CB ns: MGC10.240.41.137@tcp lock: 000000009755086f/0x543108aef6b151cc lrc: 3/0,0
### final lock_put on destroyed lock, freeing it. ns: MGC10.240.41.137@tcp lock: 000000009755086f/0x543108aef6b151cc lrc: 0/0,0
^^^ client lock gone

### MGC cancel CB ns: MGC10.240.41.137@tcp lock: 0000000068eb8698/0x543108aef6b151c5 lrc: 3/0,0
## final lock_put on destroyed lock, freeing it. ns: MGC10.240.41.137@tcp lock: 0000000068eb8698/0x543108aef6b151c5 lrc: 0/0,0
^^^ 'sptlrpc' lock gone

new lock: 0000000068eb8698/0x543108aef6b15274 lrc: 3/1,0 - client
new lock: 000000009755086f/0x543108aef6b1527b lrc: 3/1,0 - sptlrpc

### MGC cancel CB ns: MGC10.240.41.137@tcp lock: 000000009755086f/0x543108aef6b1527b lrc: 3/0,0
### final lock_put on destroyed lock, freeing it. ns: MGC10.240.41.137@tcp lock: 000000009755086f/0x543108aef6b1527b lrc: 0/0,0
^^^ sptlrpc lock gone

### MGC cancel CB ns: MGC10.240.41.137@tcp lock: 0000000068eb8698/0x543108aef6b15274 lrc: 3/0,0
### final lock_put on destroyed lock, freeing it. ns: MGC10.240.41.137@tcp lock: 0000000068eb8698/0x543108aef6b15274 lrc: 0/0,0
^^^ 'client' lock gone

dropping all unused locks from namespace MGC10.240.41.137@tcp <--- test clear MGS namespace explicitly
ldlm_lock_decref(CR) ns: MGC10.240.41.137@tcp lock: 00000000e3c67f22/0x543108aef6b15219 lrc: 3/1,0
ldlm_lock_decref(CR) ns: MGC10.240.41.137@tcp lock: 0000000074b7b922/0x543108aef6b15220 lrc: 3/1,0
^^^ last two locks are canceled - params and cliir

### MGC cancel CB ns: MGC10.240.41.137@tcp lock: 00000000e3c67f22/0x543108aef6b15219 lrc: 3/0,0
### final lock_put on destroyed lock, freeing it. ns: MGC10.240.41.137@tcp lock: 00000000e3c67f22/0x543108aef6b15219 lrc: 0/0,0
### MGC cancel CB ns: MGC10.240.41.137@tcp lock: 0000000074b7b922/0x543108aef6b15220 lrc: 3/0,0
### final lock_put on destroyed lock, freeing it. ns: MGC10.240.41.137@tcp lock: 0000000074b7b922/0x543108aef6b15220 lrc: 0/0,0

so all 4 config locks were canceled upon umount and then all 4 were occurred after mount again. The problem is that two lock were canceled and not yet re-queued when test checks locks count, so it finds only 2 locks though there would be 4 in normal conditions.

This is test issue probably related to exascale mount process which differs from master, anyway test can't prove amount of locks count is stable when it takes it. Technically test should make sure none of these config locks are being re-queued at the moment. Probably just pause after mount would be enough, to make sure all configs were processed and all is settled.

So at the moment this bug, as it described, exists only in b2_14. Though test itself in master and exascale might need update to make sure lock count is stable

The failure occurred constantly on master branch:
https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/28d34746-1e90-43af-a46c-eaca630d6bf5
https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/db65cc6c-55fe-43b6-85dc-4c8eb8c3f9ea
https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/4d34cabd-5697-4100-b2b2-63f4d0d3b6ec
